questions.question: Which state allowed licensing for individual bars and then cancelled it after public criticism?
questions.explanation: Rule 13.11 (licensing for individual bar) of Uttarakhand Excise Policy Rules 2023 was introduced by the state government to allow home owners for maintaining mini-bars. The excise policy manual 2023-24 issued made a provision for granting licences on a payment of Rs 12,000 per annum for such mini home bars to anyone who has filed Income Tax Returns for five years. It was cancelled by the state government after public criticism.

questions.question: Kulasekarapattinam, associated with ISRO’s second spaceport, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The second spaceport of Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), will come up in about two years at Kulasekarapattinam in Thoothukudi district of Tamil Nadu. It will be dedicated exclusively for Small Satellite Launch Vehicles (SSLVs) developed by the private sector. About 2,000 acres has been acquired and handed over to ISRO.

questions.question: Which state announced a new Samadhan scheme with waivers and concessions for traders?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu government announced a new Samadhan scheme with waivers and concessions for the settlement of the pending tax dues of about ₹25,000 crore, of which a majority are under the erstwhile Tamil Nadu Value Added Tax and other legacy tax legislations. The new Samadhan scheme, seeks to put an end to the pending tax arrears, disputes and appeals between the traders and the State commercial tax department.

questions.question: Yangtze River, which was seen in the news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: The World Meteorological Organization recently stated that climate change is causing an imbalance in the hydrological cycle and urged a fundamental change in policies to enhance monitoring. The organization revealed that more than 50% of global catchment areas were witnessing deviations from typical river discharge patterns, with many of them being drier than usual, citing China’s Yangtze River as an example.

questions.question: Which country played the host of the 8th BRICS International Competition Conference 2023?
questions.explanation: The 8th BRICS International Competition Conference 2023 (BRICS ICC 2023), was hosted by the Competition Commission of India (CCI) in New Delhi. Joint Statement signed by the BRICS competition authorities called for alignment towards fostering competition-driven growth and socio-economic progress. CCI handed over the baton to South Africa for the 9th BRICS International Competition Conference to be held in 2025.

questions.question: Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust (PRGT) is associated with which institution?
questions.explanation: As part of its Global Policy Agenda unveiled at Marrakesh, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has called for urgent support from members to increase its quota resources and to secure funding for the Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust (PRGT) and Resilience and Sustainability Trust (RST). The PRGT and RST are concessional lending facilities of the IMF. The PRGT is available to low-income countries that are eligible for the IMF’s concessional financing for poverty reduction, while the RST aims to help low-income and vulnerable middle-income countries build resilience to climate change and other external risks.

questions.question: Which country’s cricket team defeated Australia by 134 runs in World Cup?
questions.explanation: South Africa defeated the five-time champion Australia by 134 runs in World Cup and handed them one of the biggest losses. The South African team scored 311/7 after Quinton de Kock scored a century, his second of the tournament. While Australia were bowled out for 177 in 40.5 overs.

questions.question: Raunak Sadhwani, who was seen in the news, plays which sports?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra’s youngest Grandmaster, 17-year-old Raunak Sadhwani emerged as the U-20 World Junior Rapid Chess champion in Italy. He scored sole 8.5/11 and was also placed second in the Blitz Open event. Raunak earlier won 38th European Club Cup 2023 Open for Offerspill Sjakklubb along with Magnus Carlsen and Pranav Venkatesh.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry has introduced ‘Tradeable Green Credit special programme’?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change

questions.explanation: The Environment, Forest, and Climate Change Ministry has introduced a Green Credit programme, where an individual or entity can earn green credit and trade it on a dedicated exchange. Green Credit refers to a unit of an incentive provided for a specified activity; delivering a positive impact on the environment. This programme is a follow-up action of the ‘LiFE’- (Lifestyle for Environment) campaign. The new programme is voluntary.

questions.question: The UK government has collaborated with which Indian state for its ‘Smart district’ project?
questions.explanation: The U.K. and Tamil Nadu collaborate on a smart technology project for sustainable water, waste, and resource management. The UK-backed ‘Smart district’ project aims to address global sustainability challenges by deploying sensors across the district, gathering real-time data on water and waste management, and environmental quality in the state.
